paper.title: From Agent Loops to Deterministic Graphs: Execution Lineage for Reproducible AI-Native Work

paper_claims_verified.claim: Execution lineage (DAG of artifact-producing computations with explicit dependencies, stable intermediate boundaries, identity-based replay) prevents unrelated-branch contamination
paper_claims_verified.caution: Paper tested on controlled policy-memo tasks, not multi-agent governance systems. Scale and failure-mode differences may affect generalizability.

paper_claims_verified.claim: DAG replay preserved final memo exactly in unrelated-branch updates with zero churn and zero contamination
paper_claims_verified.caution: Our system has more artifact types (code, config, schema, data) than the paper's text memos. Preservation guarantees may be harder for non-text artifacts.

paper_claims_verified.claim: Final answer quality and maintained-state quality are distinct
paper_claims_verified.caution: This is the key insight. Our system already has evidence of this: agents report 'fixed' while typecheck fails, graph packets claim completion when work is in-progress, heartbeat signing paths exist but are never called at runtime.

paper_claims_verified.claim: Strong loop baselines can remain competitive at producing polished final outputs but mask partial state inconsistency
paper_claims_verified.caution: Our agent loops frequently produce acceptable immediate outputs while leaving stale graph packets, outdated sovereignty reports, and inconsistent cross-lane state.

execution_lineage_dag.paper_definition: DAG of artifact-producing computations with explicit dependencies
execution_lineage_dag.system_equivalent: Nexus graph / authority graph / lane evidence graph
execution_lineage_dag.gap: Our graph represents knowledge structure, not execution lineage. Adding lineage would require recording which computation produced each artifact, with what inputs.

artifact_producing_computation.paper_definition: A node in the DAG that takes inputs and produces outputs
artifact_producing_computation.gap: Lane tasks produce outputs but currently do not record their input artifact hashes or replay conditions.

explicit_dependencies.paper_definition: Edges in the DAG showing which artifacts feed which computations
explicit_dependencies.system_equivalent: Authority edges / graph edges / verification evidence
explicit_dependencies.gap: Our authority edges represent semantic relationships (VERIFIES, DERIVES_FROM) but not build/replay dependencies. A VERIFIES edge does not tell you which input hash was used.

stable_intermediate_boundaries.paper_definition: Intermediate artifacts that act as stable checkpoints, not overwritten by unrelated changes
stable_intermediate_boundaries.system_equivalent: Graph packets, store-journal snapshots, convergence bundles
stable_intermediate_boundaries.gap: Our intermediates exist but lack hash identity. Graph packets do not record which snapshot/script/schema produced them, so staleness is undetectable without manual comparison.

identity_based_replay.paper_definition: Re-running a computation from its recorded inputs produces the same output
identity_based_replay.system_equivalent: Signed messages, session IDs, phenotype IDs, artifact hashes
identity_based_replay.gap: We sign messages for authentication, not for replay. No artifact currently records a replay_command or input_hashes that would allow deterministic regeneration.

unrelated_branch_contamination.paper_definition: Changes in one branch of the DAG should not affect artifacts in unrelated branches
unrelated_branch_contamination.gap: Our contamination controls are prompt-based (allowed-files in agent instructions) not machine-checkable. The paper provides a formal basis for making scope boundaries verifiable.

maintained_state_quality.paper_definition: Quality of the persistent state between revisions, distinct from final output quality
maintained_state_quality.system_equivalent: Sovereignty reports, graph state consistency, cross-lane message integrity
maintained_state_quality.gap: We have no formal metric for this. Sovereignty reports check individual claims but do not assess whether the overall maintained state is consistent (e.g., graph packets match current code, heartbeat is wired correctly).

current_system_matches.stale_if: source code or site-index changes after generation without rebuild
upstream artifact changes invalidate the task's assumptions
graphology API changes or graph-types.ts changes without re-test

gaps_from_recent_work.gap: No staleness detection for graph packets
gaps_from_recent_work.example_from_recent_work: Graph rendering shows empty/off-screen because graph-analysis-packets.json may be stale after code changes to GraphCanvas.tsx or graph-types.ts
gaps_from_recent_work.proposed_fix: Add lineage block to graph-analysis-packets.json with source_snapshot_hash, builder_script_hash, schema_hash. Check on load.

gaps_from_recent_work.gap: No contamination control between agent tasks
gaps_from_recent_work.example_from_recent_work: Agent fixing graph rendering also touched unrelated files because allowed-files scope is prompt-based not enforced
gaps_from_recent_work.proposed_fix: Machine-checkable scope boundaries derived from execution lineage DAG. Agent task declares affected_artifacts, system verifies no write outside that set.

gaps_from_recent_work.example_from_recent_work: Agent reports 'graph fixed' but typecheck was not run, or graph packet claims fix complete when only in-progress
gaps_from_recent_work.proposed_fix: Convergence gate reports both final_answer_quality and maintained_state_quality as separate dimensions.

validation_rules: artifact_id must be unique and stable
inputs[].hash must be SHA-256 of file content at time of computation
outputs[].hash must be SHA-256 of file content after computation
status must be one of: produced, verified, replay_needed, stale, conflicted, quarantined
replay_required_when must enumerate conditions that trigger replay
preservation.unchanged_verified requires hash_before == hash_after for each entry
dependencies.upstream and downstream must reference existing artifact_ids

should_not_change.reason: graph packet build is read-only on source index
fix is implementation, not spec change

integration_points.change: Add lineage block with source_snapshot_hash, builder_script_hash, schema_hash, replay_command
integration_points.reason: Most immediate staleness risk — graph rendering depends on this file

integration_points.change: Add lineage.inputs (file hashes / message ids), lineage.outputs (paths written), lineage.downstream_replay_needed, lineage.unaffected_preserved
integration_points.reason: Work tracking is the foundation for all downstream lineage

integration_points.change: Report both final_answer_quality and maintained_state_quality as separate dimensions; list stale_artifacts and replay_required
integration_points.reason: Directly addresses the paper's key insight that these are distinct quality dimensions

integration_points.change: Add input_artifact_hashes and produced_artifact_hashes to message body
integration_points.reason: Enables cross-lane staleness detection but requires all lanes to adopt

integration_points.change: Add lineage references so review agents know which artifacts are current and which are stale
integration_points.reason: Review quality improves when agents can verify artifact freshness

recommendation_rationale: Graph packets are the most immediate pain point (empty/off-screen graphs are a maintained-state quality failure). Implementation is narrow (add lineage block to one JSON file), testable (verify hash mismatches trigger rebuild), and demonstrates the pattern before wider adoption. Store-journal and convergence gate can follow.
library_verification_note: This artifact is a Library evidence document, not a ratified proposal. It verifies that: (1) the paper exists at the stated URL, (2) its abstract claims match the user's summary, (3) the concept mapping to our system is accurate per our codebase. Ratification of any proposed protocol changes requires Archivist action via the convergence protocol.
